ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tasks" (
  "task_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each task */,
  "task_name" TEXT /* Name of the task */,
  "setup_time" INTEGER /* Setup time for the task in minutes */,
  "per_unit_time" INTEGER /* Production time per unit for the task in minutes */,
  "min_lot_size" INTEGER /* Minimum lot size for the task */,
  "max_lot_size" INTEGER /* Maximum lot size for the task */,
  "is_delivery_task" INTEGER /* Whether the task is a delivery task */,
  "machine_id" TEXT /* ID of the machine used for the task */,
  "operator_id" TEXT /* ID of the operator assigned to the task */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the task */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the task */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("task_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parts" (
  "part_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each part */,
  "part_name" TEXT /* Name of the part */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the part */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the part */,
  "weight" REAL /* Weight of the part */,
  "dimension" TEXT /* Dimensions of the part */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("part_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_workflows" (
  "workflow_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each workflow */,
  "workflow_name" TEXT /* Name of the workflow */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the workflow */,
  "estimated_duration" INTEGER /* Estimated duration of the workflow in minutes */,
  "actual_duration" INTEGER /* Actual duration of the workflow in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("workflow_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "task_parts" (
  "task_id" TEXT /* ID of the task */,
  "part_id" TEXT /* ID of the part */,
  "production_rate" INTEGER /* Production rate for the task and part combination */,
  "required_quantity" INTEGER /* Required quantity of the part for the task */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("task_id", "part_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "task_workflows" (
  "task_id" TEXT /* ID of the task */,
  "workflow_id" INTEGER /* ID of the workflow */,
  "sequence" INTEGER /* Sequence of the task in the workflow */,
  "dependency_task_id" TEXT /* ID of the task that must be completed before this task can start */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("task_id", "workflow_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inventories" (
  "part_id" TEXT /* ID of the part */,
  "inventory_level" INTEGER /* Current inventory level */,
  "reorder_point" INTEGER /* Reorder point for the part */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the inventory */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the inventory level was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("part_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_runs" (
  "run_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each production run */,
  "task_id" TEXT /* ID of the task */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the production run */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the production run */,
  "production_quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity produced during the run */,
  "operator_id" TEXT /* ID of the operator who managed the production run */,
  "machine_id" TEXT /* ID of the machine used in the production run */,
  "quality_status" TEXT /* Quality status of the production run */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("run_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_production_runs_task_id FOREIGN KEY ("task_id") REFERENCES tasks ("task_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., production manager, quality control specialist) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of the access event */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the browser used for access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "task_id" TEXT /* ID of the task */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., production rate, lead time) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "metric_date" TEXT /* Date the metric was recorded */,
  "shift" TEXT /* Shift during which the metric was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_production_metrics_task_id FOREIGN KEY ("task_id") REFERENCES tasks ("task_id")
)
